metadata.title: The Capstone Map: One System from Ten Assignments
metadata.playlist: Make It Count — The Complete Brand System
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Capstone — Complete Brand System (200 pts, due 2026-08-14)

beats.narration_text: Ciao — this is Liam. Episode 1 of 6. The capstone is not an assignment — it is an audit. The question you are answering is not 'did I do the work' — it is 'would this hold up in front of a client?' Ten assignments. One coherent brand system. This episode maps what you already built, names the six required core pieces, and lays out the pro presentation structure that the grader is actually measuring. No new deliverables today — just the map so you know what you are walking into. Let's go.

props.segment: The Capstone Map: One System from Ten Assignments
props.command: Audit my A1–A10 outputs against the six required capstone pieces. Flag anything missing.
props.runningText: integration not creation — would this hold up in front of a client?
output: Six required core pieces named and sourced
Inventory map: which assignment each piece came from
The six-part pro presentation structure with point weights

beats.act: the client-standard — coherent brand system vs scattered deliverables
beats.narration_text: The client-standard. A coherent brand system is not a collection of files — it is a set of pieces that reference each other. Same palette, same type, same voice, same positioning. A hiring manager or investor can pick up any one piece and recognize the brand without a label. Scattered deliverables are different: each assignment done well but not speaking to each other. Different type on the website and the deck. A positioning statement that does not match the social captions. A tool with a name that has nothing to do with the brand story. The capstone has six required core pieces. One: a live website. Two: a complete identity system — logo, palette, type, style guide. Three: a strategy document — UVP, pillars, personas, competitive positioning. Four: a path-specific bundle — either two resumes plus LinkedIn plus portfolio, or a pitch deck plus LinkedIn plus tool documentation. Five: three to five social posts plus two brand stories. Six: a working tool demo with the methodology behind it. Six pieces that need to feel like they came from the same brand.

props.artifactTitle: Coherent brand system — six required pieces
props.artifactHeading: Not a file collection — a system that references itself
artifactLines: 1. Live website — deployed, testable from a device you don't own.
2. Identity system — logo + palette (hex) + primary/secondary type + style guide.
3. Strategy doc — UVP · 3–5 pillars · 2+ personas · competitive positioning.
4. Path-specific bundle — Path A: 2 resumes + LinkedIn + portfolio · Path B: pitch deck + LinkedIn + tool docs.
5. Social + stories — 3–5 posts + 2 brand stories.
6. Working tool demo — live link or credentials + methodology stated.
props.sparkLine: Same palette, same voice, same positioning — all six pieces say the same brand.

beats.act: inventory map — where each piece came from
beats.narration_text: The inventory map. Each of the six pieces has an assignment source. You are not building from scratch — you are assembling what you already made. The live website came from Assignment 8 and was shaped by the visual identity in Assignment 7. The identity system came from Assignment 7 — the logo, palette, type, and style guide you built there is the yardstick every other piece gets measured against. The strategy document came from Assignments 6 and 5 — the UVP, pillars, personas, and competitive analysis. The social posts and brand stories came from Assignments 9 and 8 — the storytelling work and the social content you produced. The path-specific bundle came from Assignment 8 for resumes and portfolios or for the pitch deck frame, and the tool documentation you started in Assignments 3, 4, and 5B. The working tool is the AI tool you have been building all semester — the methodology behind it is what you have been articulating since Assignment 3. The capstone audit is not about production — it is about whether these six sources actually talk to each other.

props.artifactHeading: Assembly, not creation — trace each piece back
artifactLines: Website ← A8/A7 (visual identity shapes the site).
Identity system ← A7 (logo · palette · type · style guide — the yardstick).
Strategy doc ← A6/A5 (UVP · pillars · personas · competitive positioning).
Social + stories ← A9/A8 (brand storytelling + social content).
Path bundle ← A8 (resumes/pitch deck frame) + A3/A4/A5B (tool docs).
Working tool ← all semester — methodology articulated since A3.
props.sparkLine: Each piece has a source. The capstone asks: do they still agree?

beats.narration_text: The presentation structure. Ten minutes. Six sections. The grader is not evaluating your feelings about your brand — they are evaluating whether you can present a brand system the way a professional does. Section one: Brand Introduction, one minute. Your name, your positioning statement, one-slide brand essence. Section two: Strategy and Visual Identity, two minutes each. First the audience, UVP, pillars, and competitive positioning. Then the logo variations, palette, type, website walkthrough, and social examples. Section three: Brand Storytelling, two minutes. Present one story in full — name the framework and explain why you chose it — then connect it to your brand values. Section four: Tool Demo, three minutes. Live demonstration of two or three key features, the methodology behind the tool, and the slide that frames the demo. Each section has a time budget. Going over ten minutes costs points. Building to nine thirty is the target. Episodes 4 and 5 cover the deck and rehearsal in detail. For now — understand the structure and where each of your six pieces lives inside it.

artifactLines: 1. Brand Intro (1 min) — name · positioning statement · brand essence slide.
2. Strategy (2 min) — audience · UVP · pillars · competitive positioning.
3. Visual Identity (2 min) — logo variations · palette · type · site walkthrough · social.
4. Brand Storytelling (2 min) — one story in full · name the framework · connect to values.
5. Tool Demo (3 min) — live 2–3 features · methodology · framing slide.
Over 10 min = points off. Target: 9:30.
props.sparkLine: Structure is not a constraint — it is what makes the work legible to a client.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for The Capstone Map. The capstone is integration, not creation — the work exists. The question is whether the six pieces agree with each other. Six required core pieces: live website, complete identity system, strategy document, path-specific bundle, social posts plus brand stories, and a working tool demo with methodology. The inventory map traces each piece back to its source assignment — A3 through A9. The pro presentation structure is six sections in ten minutes, with the largest chunk given to the tool demo. Target nine thirty. Episode 2 is website and demo readiness. Episode 3 is assembly and coherence audit. Build the inventory before you watch Episode 2.

props.artifactTitle: The Capstone Map: One System from Ten Assignments
artifactLines: Capstone = assembly audit: do the six pieces agree with each other?
Six pieces: website · identity · strategy · path bundle · social+stories · tool demo.
Every piece traces back to A3–A9 — nothing is new production.
Pro structure: 6 sections · 10 min cap · target 9:30.
Tool demo gets the largest single budget: 3 minutes.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Before you watch Episode 2, run the inventory audit. Paste this prompt into Claude with your own answers filled in. The prompt reads: I am preparing for the INFO 7375 capstone presentation. I need to audit my completed assignments against the six required capstone pieces. Here is what I have built across the semester. For each item, I will tell you the assignment it came from and whether the file or link is complete. Website: [paste your deployed URL or 'not deployed yet']. Identity system from Assignment 7: [describe what you have — logo file, palette hex values, type choices, style guide page count or status]. Strategy document from Assignments 5 and 6: [describe UVP, pillars count, persona count, whether competitive section is complete]. Path-specific bundle — my path is A or B: [list what you have: resumes, LinkedIn status, portfolio samples for Path A; or pitch deck, LinkedIn, tool docs for Path B]. Social posts and brand stories from Assignment 9: [list how many posts and how many stories are complete]. Tool demo: [paste the live link or describe the demo method and which features work]. Against this inventory, tell me: which of the six required pieces is missing or incomplete, which pieces are likely to feel inconsistent with each other based on what I have described, and what is the single highest-risk gap I need to close before presentation day. After you get the audit, write down the one gap Claude flags as highest-risk and fix it before Episode 3.

props.segment: The Capstone Map: One System from Ten Assignments
props.command: Audit my A1–A10 outputs: website URL · identity system status · strategy doc status · path bundle (A or B) · social+stories count · tool demo link. Flag: missing pieces · likely inconsistencies · single highest-risk gap.
props.runningText: write down the highest-risk gap Claude flags — fix it before Episode 3…

beats.narration_text: The Capstone Map: One System from Ten Assignments. Liam, in for Bear.
